task_desc: Fix caretaker agent environment state mismatch, macOS date bug, and cleanup. Four issues in the Phase 8 caretaker pipeline that break the autonomous agent loop: (1) caretaker-bridge.js getState() reports BRAIN.stimulate.lightLevel (float: 1.0=bright, 0.5=dim, 0.0=dark) and BRAIN.stimulate.temperature (float: 0.5=neutral, 0.75=warm, 0.25=cool), but the policy (agent/caretaker-policy.md) state schema and all rule conditions expect integer indices (0=bright, 1=dim, 2=dark for light; 0=neutral, 1=warm, 2=cool for temp). This inverts the policy logic: bright light (1.0) is read as "dim" (index 1), dark light (0.0) as "bright" (index 0). Policy rules 2 (stressor stacking against bright) and 5 (dim lights for fatigue) never fire correctly. Fix: change getState() to report lightStateIndex and tempStateIndex instead of the raw float values, and update the set_light/set_temp handlers to also validate incoming commands against the index scheme. (2) agent/run.sh line 134 uses date +%s%3N for millisecond timestamps, but macOS BSD date does not support %N (GNU extension). CURRENT_TIME becomes literal "1711548000%3N" instead of a number, breaking policy rule 6 (idle > 120s stimulation) because the LLM receives a garbage timestamp. Fix: use $(python3 -c "import time; print(int(time.time()*1000))") or $(date +%s)000 as a portable alternative. (3) caretaker-decisions.log is written by agent/run.sh but not in .gitignore (only caretaker.log is excluded). Add it. (4) caretaker-bridge.js retries WebSocket connections to ws://localhost:7600 every 3 seconds indefinitely on iOS (WKWebView/file:// context) where no server exists, wasting CPU/battery. Fix: skip connection when location.protocol is "file:". Files: js/caretaker-bridge.js (getState environment fields, file:// guard in init), agent/caretaker-policy.md (state schema docs), agent/run.sh (date command), .gitignore (add caretaker-decisions.log).
